sanskrit: धृतराष्ट्र उवाच | धर्मक्षेत्रे कुरुक्षेत्रे समवेता युयुत्सवः | मामकाः पाण्डवाश्चैव किमकुर्वत सञ्जय ||
hindi: धृतराष्ट्र ने पूछा — हे संजय! धर्मभूमि कुरुक्षेत्र में एकत्रित मेरे और पाण्डु के पुत्रों ने क्या किया?
english: Dhritarashtra said: O Sanjay, what did my sons and the sons of Pandu do when they assembled on the holy field of Kurukshetra, eager to fight?
insight: Every moral crisis begins with a question — what are the righteous and the unrighteous forces doing within me?

sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाच | दृष्ट्वा तु पाण्डवानीकं व्यूढं दुर्योधनस्तदा | आचार्यमुपसङ्गम्य राजा वचनमब्रवीत् ||
hindi: संजय बोले — पाण्डव सेना की व्यूह रचना देखकर राजा दुर्योधन आचार्य द्रोण के पास गया और बोला।
english: Sanjay said: On seeing the Pandava army in battle formation, King Duryodhana approached his teacher Dronacharya and spoke these words.
insight: Pride before a battle often masks fear — Duryodhana seeks reassurance, not wisdom.

sanskrit: पश्यैतां पाण्डुपुत्राणामाचार्य महतीं चमूम् | व्यूढां द्रुपदपुत्रेण तव शिष्येण धीमता ||
hindi: हे आचार्य! पाण्डु-पुत्रों की इस विशाल सेना को देखिए, जिसे आपके बुद्धिमान शिष्य द्रुपद-पुत्र ने व्यूहबद्ध किया है।
english: O Teacher, behold this great army of the sons of Pandu, arrayed by your talented disciple, the son of Drupada.
insight: A student's strength is a tribute to the teacher — even enemies acknowledge each other's abilities on the battlefield.

sanskrit: अत्र शूरा महेष्वासा भीमार्जुनसमा युधि | युयुधानो विराटश्च द्रुपदश्च महारथः ||
hindi: इस सेना में महान धनुर्धारी हैं — भीम और अर्जुन के समान युद्ध करने वाले, युयुधान, विराट और महारथी द्रुपद।
english: Here are great archers equal in battle to Bhima and Arjuna — Yuyudhana, Virata, and the great chariot-warrior Drupada.
insight: Respect your adversary's strength — underestimating an opponent is the first step toward defeat.

sanskrit: धृष्टकेतुश्चेकितानः काशिराजश्च वीर्यवान् | पुरुजित्कुन्तिभोजश्च शैब्यश्च नरपुङ्गवः ||
hindi: धृष्टकेतु, चेकितान, पराक्रमी काशिराज, पुरुजित, कुन्तिभोज और मनुष्यों में श्रेष्ठ शैब्य भी हैं।
english: Also Dhrishtaketu, Chekitana, the valiant King of Kashi, Purujit, Kuntibhoja, and Shaibya, the best of men.
insight: Every great battle is fought by named individuals with unique stories — history is always personal before it is collective.

sanskrit: युधामन्युश्च विक्रान्त उत्तमौजाश्च वीर्यवान् | सौभद्रो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्व एव महारथाः ||
hindi: पराक्रमी युधामन्यु, बलवान उत्तमौजा, सुभद्रापुत्र और द्रौपदी के पुत्र — ये सभी महारथी हैं।
english: The valiant Yudhamanyu, the powerful Uttamaujas, the son of Subhadra, and the sons of Draupadi — all are great warriors.
insight: Every warrior has a lineage — knowing who you come from shapes how you fight and what you fight for.

sanskrit: अस्माकं तु विशिष्टा ये तान्निबोध द्विजोत्तम | नायका मम सैन्यस्य संज्ञार्थं तान्ब्रवीमि ते ||
hindi: हे द्विजोत्तम! हमारी तरफ के भी विशिष्ट योद्धाओं को जानिए — मैं अपनी सेना के नायकों को आपको बताता हूँ।
english: O best of Brahmins, know also the distinguished commanders on our side. For your information, let me name the generals of my army.
insight: A leader must know their own people as well as they know the enemy — self-knowledge is strategic wisdom.

sanskrit: भवान्भीष्मश्च कर्णश्च कृपश्च समितिञ्जयः | अश्वत्थामा विकर्णश्च सौमदत्तिस्तथैव च ||
hindi: आप (द्रोण), भीष्म, कर्ण, संग्राम-विजयी कृप, अश्वत्थामा, विकर्ण और सोमदत्त के पुत्र।
english: Yourself (Drona), Bhishma, Karna, and Kripa the ever-victorious in battle; Ashwatthama, Vikarna, and also the son of Somadatta.
insight: Greatness surrounds you — those beside you in battle define the moral weight of the cause you serve.

sanskrit: अन्ये च बहवः शूरा मदर्थे त्यक्तजीविताः | नानाशस्त्रप्रहरणाः सर्वे युद्धविशारदाः ||
hindi: और भी अनेक शूर-वीर हैं जो मेरे लिए अपने प्राण न्योछावर करने को तैयार हैं — विभिन्न अस्त्र-शस्त्रों से सज्जित और युद्ध-कुशल।
english: And many other brave warriors are ready to sacrifice their lives for my sake, armed with various weapons and all skilled in warfare.
insight: Loyalty earned through respect is worth more than loyalty bought through fear.

sanskrit: अपर्याप्तं तदस्माकं बलं भीष्माभिरक्षितम् | पर्याप्तं त्विदमेतेषां बलं भीमाभिरक्षितम् ||
hindi: भीष्म द्वारा रक्षित हमारी सेना अपर्याप्त है, जबकि भीम द्वारा रक्षित उनकी सेना पर्याप्त है।
english: Our army protected by Bhishma is insufficient, while their army protected by Bhima is sufficient.
insight: Duryodhana's doubt reveals that external strength means nothing when inner confidence is absent.

sanskrit: अयनेषु च सर्वेषु यथाभागमवस्थिताः | भीष्ममेवाभिरक्षन्तु भवन्तः सर्व एव हि ||
hindi: इसलिए सभी अपने-अपने स्थान पर खड़े होकर सब मिलकर भीष्म की रक्षा करें।
english: Therefore, all of you stationed at your respective positions must all protect Bhishma alone.
insight: True leadership requires protecting wisdom — Bhishma represents the last moral anchor on the wrong side.

sanskrit: तस्य सञ्जनयन्हर्षं कुरुवृद्धः पितामहः | सिंहनादं विनद्योच्चैः शङ्खं दध्मौ प्रतापवान् ||
hindi: कुरुकुल के वृद्ध, प्रतापी पितामह भीष्म ने दुर्योधन को प्रसन्न करने के लिए सिंह के समान गर्जना करते हुए शंख बजाया।
english: Then the glorious grandsire of the Kurus, the oldest member, Bhishma, blew his conch loudly like a lion's roar, bringing joy to Duryodhana.
insight: Even the greatest elder can be swept into a wrong cause by loyalty — wisdom must be paired with moral courage.

sanskrit: ततः शङ्खाश्च भेर्यश्च पणवानकगोमुखाः | सहसैवाभ्यहन्यन्त स तु शब्दस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ||
hindi: तब एक साथ शंख, नगाड़े, ढोल, मृदंग और तुरही बज उठे — वह शब्द अत्यंत कोलाहलपूर्ण हो गया।
english: Then suddenly, conches, kettledrums, tabors, trumpets, and cow-horns blared forth together. That sound became tumultuous.
insight: War announces itself with noise and ceremony — underneath the pageantry, silence would be more honest.

sanskrit: ततः श्वेतैर्हयैर्युक्ते महति स्यन्दने स्थितौ | माधवः पाण्डवश्चैव दिव्यौ शङ्खौ प्रदध्मतुः ||
hindi: तब सफेद घोड़ों से जुड़े महान रथ पर सवार माधव (कृष्ण) और पाण्डव (अर्जुन) ने अपने-अपने दिव्य शंख बजाए।
english: Then, from the great chariot yoked with white horses, Madhava (Krishna) and Pandava (Arjuna) blew their divine conches.
insight: Divinity enters the battlefield not to destroy but to guide — Krishna's conch signals divine wisdom, not war.

sanskrit: पाञ्चजन्यं हृषीकेशो देवदत्तं धनञ्जयः | पौण्ड्रं दध्मौ महाशङ्खं भीमकर्मा वृकोदरः ||
hindi: हृषीकेश (कृष्ण) ने पाञ्चजन्य, धनंजय (अर्जुन) ने देवदत्त और भयंकर कर्म करने वाले भीम ने पौण्ड्र शंख बजाया।
english: Hrishikesha (Krishna) blew the Panchajanya, Dhananjaya the Devadatta, and Bhima of terrible deeds blew the great conch Paundra.
insight: Each warrior has their own voice — find yours and blow it with full strength before the battle begins.

sanskrit: अनन्तविजयं राजा कुन्तीपुत्रो युधिष्ठिरः | नकुलः सहदेवश्च सुघोषमणिपुष्पकौ ||
hindi: कुन्ती-पुत्र राजा युधिष्ठिर ने अनन्तविजय और नकुल व सहदेव ने सुघोष और मणिपुष्पक शंख बजाए।
english: King Yudhishthira, the son of Kunti, blew the Anantavijaya, and Nakula and Sahadeva blew their conches Sughosha and Manipushpaka.
insight: Every member of a righteous team has a distinct role — unity does not mean uniformity.

sanskrit: काश्यश्च परमेष्वासः शिखण्डी च महारथः | धृष्टद्युम्नो विराटश्च सात्यकिश्चापराजितः ||
hindi: श्रेष्ठ धनुर्धारी काशिराज, महारथी शिखंडी, धृष्टद्युम्न, विराट और अजेय सात्यकि।
english: The King of Kashi, foremost among archers, the great chariot-warrior Shikhandi, Dhrishtadyumna, Virata, and the undefeated Satyaki.
insight: An army of principle is powerful — every righteous warrior adds to the moral weight of the cause.

sanskrit: द्रुपदो द्रौपदेयाश्च सर्वशः पृथिवीपते | सौभद्रश्च महाबाहुः शङ्खान्दध्मुः पृथक्पृथक् ||
hindi: हे पृथ्वीपति! द्रुपद, द्रौपदी के पुत्र और महाबाहु सुभद्रापुत्र सभी ने अलग-अलग शंख बजाए।
english: O lord of the earth, Drupada, the sons of Draupadi, and the mighty-armed son of Subhadra all blew their conches separately.
insight: Each voice in a righteous chorus matters — no contribution to truth is too small.

sanskrit: स घोषो धार्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत् | नभश्च पृथिवीं चैव तुमुलो व्यनुनादयन् ||
hindi: उस शब्द ने धृतराष्ट्र के पुत्रों के हृदय विदीर्ण कर दिए और आकाश तथा पृथ्वी को गुंजा दिया।
english: That tumultuous sound pierced the hearts of Dhritarashtra's sons, reverberating through the sky and the earth.
insight: Truth, when spoken with full force, shakes even the most hardened heart — sound is the first weapon of the righteous.

sanskrit: अथ व्यवस्थितान्दृष्ट्वा धार्तराष्ट्रान् कपिध्वजः | प्रवृत्ते शस्त्रसम्पाते धनुरुद्यम्य पाण्डवः ||
hindi: तब कपिध्वज अर्जुन ने, जब शस्त्र चलने लगे, धृतराष्ट्र के पुत्रों को व्यवस्थित देखकर धनुष उठाया।
english: Then Arjuna, son of Pandu, whose flag bore the image of Hanuman, took up his bow as the weapons were about to be discharged, and saw Dhritarashtra's sons standing arrayed.
insight: Before acting, the wise pause to observe — action without observation is reaction, not response.

sanskrit: हृषीकेशं तदा वाक्यमिदमाह महीपते | अर्जुन उवाच सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये रथं स्थापय मेऽच्युत ||
hindi: हे राजन! अर्जुन ने हृषीकेश से कहा — हे अच्युत! मेरा रथ दोनों सेनाओं के मध्य में खड़ा कीजिए।
english: O King, Arjuna then spoke to Hrishikesha: O Achyuta, please place my chariot between the two armies.
insight: Before any major decision, position yourself where you can see all sides — clarity requires perspective.

sanskrit: यावदेतान्निरीक्षेऽहं योद्धुकामानवस्थितान् | कैर्मया सह योद्धव्यमस्मिन् रणसमुद्यमे ||
hindi: जब तक मैं इन युद्ध की इच्छा रखने वाले खड़े लोगों को देख लूँ — इस युद्ध में मुझे किन-किन से लड़ना है।
english: Let me see who has come here to fight, with whom I must contend in this great trial of arms.
insight: Know your adversaries before engaging — wisdom lies in understanding before acting.

sanskrit: योत्स्यमानानवेक्षेऽहं य एतेऽत्र समागताः | धार्तराष्ट्रस्य दुर्बुद्धेर्युद्धे प्रियचिकीर्षवः ||
hindi: मैं उन लोगों को देखना चाहता हूँ जो दुर्बुद्धि दुर्योधन का युद्ध में प्रिय करने की इच्छा से यहाँ आए हैं।
english: Let me see those who have come here to serve the evil-minded Duryodhana by joining him in battle.
insight: Examining whose cause you serve is the most important question before taking any side.

sanskrit: सञ्जय उवाच | एवमुक्तो हृषीकेशो गुडाकेशेन भारत | सेनयोरुभयोर्मध्ये स्थापयित्वा रथोत्तमम् ||
hindi: संजय बोले — हे भारत! गुडाकेश (अर्जुन) के इस प्रकार कहने पर हृषीकेश ने दोनों सेनाओं के बीच में श्रेष्ठ रथ खड़ा किया।
english: Sanjay said: O Bharata, thus addressed by Gudakesha (Arjuna), Hrishikesha placed the finest chariot between the two armies.
insight: A true guide takes you exactly where you ask — even when the destination reveals painful truths.

sanskrit: भीष्मद्रोणप्रमुखतः सर्वेषां च महीक्षिताम् | उवाच पार्थ पश्यैतान्समवेतान्कुरूनिति ||
hindi: भीष्म और द्रोण के सामने तथा सभी राजाओं के सामने कृष्ण ने कहा — हे पार्थ! इन एकत्रित कुरुओं को देखो।
english: In front of Bhishma, Drona, and all the kings, Krishna said: O Partha, behold all the Kurus assembled here.
insight: Sometimes the most compassionate act is simply showing you the truth, unfiltered — then letting you decide.

sanskrit: तत्रापश्यत्स्थितान्पार्थः पितॄनथ पितामहान् | आचार्यान्मातुलान्भ्रातॄन्पुत्रान्पौत्रान्सखींस्तथा ||
hindi: वहाँ पार्थ ने पिता, पितामह, आचार्य, मामा, भाई, पुत्र, पौत्र, मित्र — इन सबको खड़ा देखा।
english: There Partha saw standing in the ranks fathers, grandfathers, teachers, maternal uncles, brothers, sons, grandsons, and friends as well.
insight: Every war is ultimately a civil war — the people on the other side were once your own people.

sanskrit: श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि | तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेयः सर्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान् ||
hindi: दोनों सेनाओं में ससुर और सुहृदों को भी देखा — उन सब बंधुओं को इस प्रकार खड़ा देखकर।
english: Also father-in-law and well-wishers in both armies. The son of Kunti, seeing all these kinsmen thus standing arranged.
insight: The hardest battles are those where love stands on both sides — attachment is the root of all moral confusion.

sanskrit: कृपया परयाविष्टो विषीदन्निदमब्रवीत् | अर्जुन उवाच दृष्ट्वेमं स्वजनं कृष्ण युयुत्सुं समुपस्थितम् ||
hindi: अत्यंत करुणा से व्याप्त और दुःखी होकर अर्जुन बोले — हे कृष्ण! युद्ध की इच्छा रखने वाले इन स्वजनों को देखकर।
english: Was overcome with great compassion and spoke in grief: O Krishna, seeing my own people standing here eager to fight.
insight: When duty clashes with attachment, even the bravest warrior's resolve crumbles — this is the beginning of wisdom.

sanskrit: सीदन्ति मम गात्राणि मुखं च परिशुष्यति | वेपथुश्च शरीरे मे रोमहर्षश्च जायते ||
hindi: मेरे अंग शिथिल हो रहे हैं, मुख सूख रहा है, शरीर में कम्पन और रोमांच हो रहा है।
english: My limbs fail and my mouth is parched, my body quivers, and my hair stands on end.
insight: The body speaks truth before the mind admits it — Arjuna's symptoms are the physical language of moral conflict.

sanskrit: गाण्डीवं स्रंसते हस्तात्त्वक्चैव परिदह्यते | न च शक्नोम्यवस्थातुं भ्रमतीव च मे मनः ||
hindi: गाण्डीव धनुष हाथ से गिर रहा है, त्वचा जल रही है, मैं खड़ा नहीं रह पा रहा और मन चक्कर खा रहा है।
english: My bow slips from my hand, my skin burns, I am unable to stand, and my mind seems to whirl.
insight: Purpose collapses when the heart is not behind the action — without inner alignment, even the greatest warrior falls.

sanskrit: निमित्तानि च पश्यामि विपरीतानि केशव | न च श्रेयोऽनुपश्यामि हत्वा स्वजनमाहवे ||
hindi: हे केशव! मैं विपरीत शकुन देख रहा हूँ और स्वजनों को मारकर मुझे कोई कल्याण नहीं दिखता।
english: O Keshava, I see only omens of misfortune. I see no good in killing my own kinsmen in battle.
insight: Sometimes refusing to fight is not cowardice but the deepest wisdom — know what you are truly fighting for.

sanskrit: न काङ्क्षे विजयं कृष्ण न च राज्यं सुखानि च | किं नो राज्येन गोविन्द किं भोगैर्जीवितेन वा ||
hindi: हे कृष्ण! मुझे न विजय की इच्छा है, न राज्य की, न सुखों की। हे गोविन्द! हमें राज्य से क्या प्रयोजन?
english: O Krishna, I do not wish for victory, nor a kingdom, nor pleasures. O Govinda, what use is a kingdom, or enjoyment, or even life itself?
insight: True grief strips away all ambition — in this moment Arjuna is more honest than all who fight for kingdoms.

sanskrit: येषामर्थे काङ्क्षितं नो राज्यं भोगाः सुखानि च | त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च ||
hindi: जिनके लिए हमें राज्य, भोग और सुख अभीष्ट हैं, वे ही ये लोग यहाँ युद्ध में प्राण और धन की आहुति देने के लिए खड़े हैं।
english: Those very persons for whose sake we desire kingdom, enjoyment, and pleasure — they are standing here ready to give up their lives and wealth.
insight: When your reason for living stands in front of you as your reason to kill, all ambition becomes hollow.

sanskrit: आचार्याः पितरः पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहाः | मातुलाः श्वशुराः पौत्राः श्यालाः सम्बन्धिनस्तथा ||
hindi: गुरुजन, पिता, पुत्र, पितामह, मामा, ससुर, पौत्र, साले और अन्य संबंधी।
english: Teachers, fathers, sons, and also grandfathers; maternal uncles, fathers-in-law, grandsons, brothers-in-law, and other relatives.
insight: War renders every human label — father, son, teacher — meaningless; only loss remains.

sanskrit: एतान्न हन्तुमिच्छामि घ्नतोऽपि मधुसूदन | अपि त्रैलोक्यराज्यस्य हेतोः किं नु महीकृते ||
hindi: हे मधुसूदन! ये यदि मुझे मारें तो भी मैं इन्हें नहीं मारना चाहता — तीनों लोकों के राज्य के लिए भी नहीं, फिर पृथ्वी के लिए तो क्या।
english: O Madhusudana, even if they should slay me, I do not wish to fight them — not even for dominion over the three worlds, let alone for this earth.
insight: The greatest moral stand is sometimes to lay down your weapons — not from weakness, but from love.

sanskrit: निहत्य धार्तराष्ट्रान्नः का प्रीतिः स्याज्जनार्दन | पापमेवाश्रयेदस्मान्हत्वैतानाततायिनः ||
hindi: हे जनार्दन! धृतराष्ट्र के पुत्रों को मारने पर हमें क्या प्रसन्नता होगी? इन आततायियों को मारने से हमें पाप ही लगेगा।
english: O Janardana, what pleasure shall we find in killing the sons of Dhritarashtra? Sin will overcome us if we slay these aggressors.
insight: Victory without virtue is just organized violence — winning the wrong war creates more suffering than losing it.

sanskrit: तस्मान्नार्हा वयं हन्तुं धार्तराष्ट्रान्स्वबान्धवान् | स्वजनं हि कथं हत्वा सुखिनः स्याम माधव ||
hindi: इसलिए हे माधव! हम अपने बंधु धृतराष्ट्र के पुत्रों को मारने के योग्य नहीं हैं। अपने ही लोगों को मारकर हम सुखी कैसे रह सकते हैं?
english: Therefore it is not proper for us to kill Dhritarashtra's sons, our own relatives. How can we be happy by killing our own kinsmen, O Madhava?
insight: Happiness built on the grief of loved ones is no happiness at all — the cost of some victories is everything worth winning.

sanskrit: यद्यप्येते न पश्यन्ति लोभोपहतचेतसः | कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं मित्रद्रोहे च पातकम् ||
hindi: यदि ये लोभ से दूषित चित्त वाले कुल-नाश का दोष और मित्र-विरोध के पाप को नहीं देख रहे।
english: Even if these men, blinded by greed, do not see the wrong in destroying their family or the sin of fighting against friends.
insight: Even when others lose their moral compass, you are not freed from your own — righteousness is never relative.

sanskrit: कथं न ज्ञेयमस्माभिः पापादस्मान्निवर्तितुम् | कुलक्षयकृतं दोषं प्रपश्यद्भिर्जनार्दन ||
hindi: हे जनार्दन! कुल-नाश के दोष को स्पष्ट देखते हुए हम लोगों को इस पाप से निवृत्त होना क्यों नहीं चाहिए?
english: Why should we not know enough to turn away from this sin, O Janardana, when we can see the wrong in the destruction of the family?
insight: Seeing the wrong clearly is the first act of wisdom — then refusing to participate is the second.

sanskrit: कुलक्षये प्रणश्यन्ति कुलधर्माः सनातनाः | धर्मे नष्टे कुलं कृत्स्नमधर्मोऽभिभवत्युत ||
hindi: कुल के नाश होने से कुल की सनातन परंपराएँ नष्ट हो जाती हैं, धर्म के नष्ट होने पर अधर्म कुल को दबा लेता है।
english: With the destruction of the family, the eternal family traditions are lost. When dharma is lost, adharma overwhelms the entire family.
insight: Society is held together by invisible threads of tradition — break the family, and you unravel civilization itself.

sanskrit: अधर्माभिभवात्कृष्ण प्रदुष्यन्ति कुलस्त्रियः | स्त्रीषु दुष्टासु वार्ष्णेय जायते वर्णसङ्करः ||
hindi: हे कृष्ण! अधर्म के प्रभाव से कुल की स्त्रियाँ दूषित हो जाती हैं और हे वार्ष्णेय! स्त्रियों के दूषित होने पर वर्ण-संकर होता है।
english: O Krishna, when adharma prevails, the women of the family become corrupted, and O Varsheya, from the corruption of women comes intermixture.
insight: Social collapse always begins with the erosion of values in the home — culture is transmitted through family.

sanskrit: सङ्करो नरकायैव कुलघ्नानां कुलस्य च | पतन्ति पितरो ह्येषां लुप्तपिण्डोदकक्रियाः ||
hindi: वर्ण-संकर कुल-नाशियों और कुल को नरक में ले जाता है। पिण्ड और जल की क्रिया के लुप्त होने पर पितर भी नरक में पड़ते हैं।
english: This intermixture brings both the destroyers of the family and the family itself to hell. The ancestors also fall, being deprived of offerings of food and water.
insight: Every generation is responsible to its ancestors and descendants — we are not isolated, but links in a chain of duty.

sanskrit: दोषैरेतैः कुलघ्नानां वर्णसङ्करकारकैः | उत्साद्यन्ते जातिधर्माः कुलधर्माश्च शाश्वताः ||
hindi: इन कुलनाशियों के इन दोषों से, जो वर्ण-संकर उत्पन्न करते हैं, जाति-धर्म और सनातन कुल-धर्म नष्ट हो जाते हैं।
english: By these misdeeds of those who destroy the family and cause confusion of duties, the eternal communal and family traditions are uprooted.
insight: Ancient structures of meaning collapse faster than we build them — destruction is always easier than creation.

sanskrit: उत्सन्नकुलधर्माणां मनुष्याणां जनार्दन | नरकेऽनियतं वासो भवतीत्यनुशुश्रुम ||
hindi: हे जनार्दन! जिनके कुल-धर्म नष्ट हो गए हैं, ऐसे मनुष्यों का नरक में वास होता है — ऐसा हमने सुना है।
english: O Janardana, we have heard that men whose family traditions are destroyed must dwell in hell for an indefinite period.
insight: Those who betray their highest values for temporary gain find themselves lost in a world without meaning — that is the truest hell.

sanskrit: अहो बत महत्पापं कर्तुं व्यवसिता वयम् | यद्राज्यसुखलोभेन हन्तुं स्वजनमुद्यताः ||
hindi: अहो! हम बड़ा पाप करने को तैयार हो रहे हैं कि राज्य-सुख के लोभ से अपने ही स्वजनों को मारने के लिए उद्यत हैं।
english: Alas, how strange it is that we have set out to perform this great sin! Driven by the desire for royal pleasures, we are ready to slay our own kinsmen.
insight: The moment of recognition — seeing the gap between who you are and what you are about to do — is the birth of conscience.

sanskrit: यदि मामप्रतीकारमशस्त्रं शस्त्रपाणयः | धार्तराष्ट्रा रणे हन्युस्तन्मे क्षेमतरं भवेत् ||
hindi: यदि अस्त्र-शस्त्र हाथ में लिए हुए धृतराष्ट्र के पुत्र युद्ध में मेरा प्रतिकार न करने वाले और निहत्थे मुझे मार दें, तो वह मेरे लिए अधिक कल्याणकारी होगा।
english: It would be better if the sons of Dhritarashtra, weapons in hand, should slay me in battle while I am unarmed and unresisting.
insight: There is nobility in choosing death over moral compromise — but whether this is wisdom or despair is the Gita's opening question.

sanskrit: एवमुक्त्वार्जुनः संख्ये रथोपस्थ उपाविशत् | विसृज्य सशरं चापं शोकसंविग्नमानसः ||
hindi: इस प्रकार कहकर, युद्धभूमि में शोक से व्याकुल मन वाले अर्जुन ने धनुष-बाण छोड़ दिया और रथ पर बैठ गए।
english: Having thus spoken, Arjuna, overwhelmed with sorrow, cast aside his bow and arrows and sat down in the chariot, his mind distressed with grief.
insight: The Gita begins where all human journeys begin — in grief, confusion, and the inability to act without wisdom.
